linting.notes: No linting configuration found. No .flake8, .pylintrc, ruff.toml, or .pre-commit-config.yaml. No linting steps in CI workflows.

setup_required: Requires either docker-compose (caikit + TGIS containers) OR KServe cluster
env_vars: ISVC_URL - Inference service URL for KServe tests (optional)
CI - Set to 'true' in CI environment (optional)
testing.mock_patterns: No mocks - only integration tests against real services
testing.notes: This repo has NO traditional unit tests. Only integration/smoke tests that require running infrastructure: (1) Docker Compose test: requires caikit-tgis-serving container + TGIS container + converted model files. Run via test/compose/smoke-test.sh. (2) KServe test: requires full Kubernetes cluster with KServe, Istio, and Knative. The smoke-test.py script tests HTTP/gRPC inference endpoints by making actual inference requests. Cannot be run in isolation without infrastructure.

ci.notes: All gating checks require container/cluster infrastructure. The build-and-test.yml workflow runs on pull_request and is the main gating workflow. It builds a Docker image, then runs smoke tests in both docker-compose and KServe environments. The kserve-test.yml runs on schedule only (not gating). No linting or unit tests in CI.

build.notes: Uses Poetry for Python dependency management (Python 3.11). The Dockerfile uses a multi-stage build with UBI9 minimal base. Project has minimal Python code (only 2 .py files: test/smoke-test.py and utils/convert.py). Main deliverable is the container image, not a Python package.

conventions.test_naming: Integration tests only: smoke-test.py (not pytest/unittest pattern)
conventions.notes: Very minimal codebase - only 2 Python source files. No traditional test file naming conventions (no test_*.py or *_test.py pattern with unit tests). The smoke test is an integration test that makes real inference requests.

container_recipe.notes: No linting configured. Tests cannot be run in a simple container - they require full infrastructure (docker-compose with TGIS container, or KServe cluster). Can only validate that Python files compile and imports work. Dependency installation takes several minutes due to large ML packages (torch, transformers, caikit).

gaps: No linting configuration or linting in CI (no flake8, ruff, black, mypy, etc.)
No traditional unit tests - only integration/smoke tests
Tests require external infrastructure (cannot run in isolation):
- Docker Compose test requires running caikit + TGIS containers
- KServe test requires full Kubernetes cluster with KServe/Istio/Knative
Cannot validate patches locally without setting up full environment
